name: Chocolate Mint Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 1/2 cup of mint chocolate chips over hot (not boiling) water, stirring until smooth. Set aside.
2. In a small bowl, combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt; set aside.
3. In a large bowl, beat butter, sugar, and vanilla extract until creamy. Beat in egg, then blend in melted chocolate chips.
4. Gradually add the flour mixture to the wet ingredients. Shape dough into a ball, wrap in waxed paper, and chill for about 1 hour.
5. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
6. On a lightly floured surface, roll dough to 1/16-inch thickness. Cut with a 2-inch cookie cutter.
7. Reroll remaining dough and cut out more cookies. Place on ungreased cookie sheets.
8. Bake for 8-10 minutes until set. Cool completely on wire racks.
9. For the glaze, combine remaining mint chocolate chips, vegetable shortening, corn syrup, and water over hot (not boiling) water, stirring until smooth.
10. Remove from heat but keep mixture over hot water. Dip half of each cookie into glaze, shaking off excess. Place cookies on waxed paper-lined cookie sheets. Chill until glaze sets (about 10 minutes). Keep refrigerated until ready to use.
